---
title: "Text Mining Movie Reviews"
categories: ["R"]
date: "6-16-2026"
draft: false
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

# Modifications... 

## Tokenize Bi-Gram

I am not seeing a lot of insight from looking at single words. 

```{r}
tokenize_text_bigram <- function(data) {
  data |>
    unnest_tokens(bigram, text_clean, token = "ngrams", n = 2) |>
    separate(bigram, into = c("word1", "word2"), sep = " ") |>
    filter(
      !word1 %in% stop_words$word,
      !word2 %in% stop_words$word
    ) |>
    unite(word, word1, word2, sep = " ")
}
```

Even with the bi-gram, I want to see what we get if we remove proper nouns. 

```{r}
dogville_tokenize_bigram <- tokenize_text_bigram(clean_dogville) 
dogville_tokenize_bigram <- dogville_tokenize_bigram |>
  filter(
    !str_detect(
      word,
      "\\b(film|movie|dogville|trier|von|kidman|nicole|lars|paul|bettany|lauren|bacall|james|caan|john|hurt|patricia|clarkson|ben|gazzara)\\b"
    )
  )
```

```{r}
sentiment_wordcloud <- function(data, lexicon = "bing") {
  sentiment_words <- get_sentiments(lexicon)

  sentiment_matrix <- data |>
    separate(word, into = c("word1", "word2"), sep = " ", remove = FALSE) |>
    left_join(
      sentiment_words,
      by = c("word1" = "word")
    ) |>
    rename(sentiment1 = sentiment) |>
    left_join(
      sentiment_words,
      by = c("word2" = "word")
    ) |>
    rename(sentiment2 = sentiment) |>
    mutate(
      sentiment = case_when(
        !is.na(sentiment1) & is.na(sentiment2) ~ sentiment1,
        is.na(sentiment1) & !is.na(sentiment2) ~ sentiment2,
        sentiment1 == sentiment2 ~ sentiment1,
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(sentiment)) |>
    count(word, sentiment) |>
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = sentiment,
      values_from = n,
      values_fill = 0
    ) |>
    column_to_rownames("word") |>
    as.matrix()

  comparison.cloud(
    sentiment_matrix,
    colors = c("#C0392B", "#2E7D32"),
    max.words = 100
  )
}
```

## Plot Term Frequencies

```{r}
dog_ville_frequencies |>
    slice_max(n, n = 8) |>
    mutate(word = reorder(word, n)) |>
    ggplot(aes(y = word, x = n)) +
    geom_col() +
    labs(
      title = "Top 8 Words",
      x = "Count"
    )
```

## Work Cloud

```{r}
sentiment_wordcloud <- function(data, lexicon = "bing") {
  sentiment_matrix <- data |>
    inner_join(get_sentiments(lexicon), by = "word") |>
    count(word, sentiment) |>
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = sentiment,
      values_from = n,
      values_fill = 0
    ) |>
    column_to_rownames("word") |>
    as.matrix()

  comparison.cloud(
    sentiment_matrix,
    colors = c("#C0392B", "#2E7D32"),
    max.words = 100
  )
}
```

## Plot Topics

```{r}
plot_topics <- function(model) {
  top_terms <- tidy(model, matrix = "beta") |>
    group_by(topic) |>
    slice_max(beta, n = 7, with_ties = FALSE) |>
    summarise(terms = paste(term, collapse = ", "))

  tidy(model, matrix = "gamma") |>
    group_by(topic) |>
    summarise(gamma = mean(gamma)) |>
    left_join(top_terms, by = "topic") |>
    mutate(topic = reorder(paste("Topic", topic), gamma)) |>
    ggplot(aes(topic, gamma, label = terms)) +
    geom_col() +
    geom_text(hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.002, size = 3) +
    coord_flip(clip = "off") +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = scales::percent,
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.7))
    ) +
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = "Average topic prevalence",
      title = "Topic Prevalence and Top Terms"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}
```

```{r}
dogville_model <- model_data(dogville_stm, k = 7)
plot_topics(dogville_model)
```


# Old

(everything below this is old..)

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidytext)
library(wordcloud)
library(readr)
```

```{r}
dogville <- read_csv("data/dogville_reviews_text_only.csv")
head(dogville)
```


# Clean Data

```{r}
clean_text <- function(data, text) {
  data |>
    mutate(
      text_clean = {{ text }} |>
        str_to_lower() |>
        str_replace_all("<br\\s*/?>", " ") |>
        str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9\\s]", " ") |>
        str_squish()
    )
}
```

```{r}
clean_dogville <- clean_text(dogville, review_text)
```

# Tokenize Text

```{r}
tokenize_text <- function(data) {
  data |>
    unnest_tokens(word, text_clean) |>
    anti_join(stop_words, by = "word")
}
```

```{r}
dogville_tokenize <- tokenize_text(clean_dogville) 
```

```{r}
tokenize_text <- function(data) {
  data |>
    unnest_tokens(bigram, text_clean, token = "ngrams", n = 2) |>
    separate(bigram, into = c("word1", "word2"), sep = " ") |>
    filter(
      !word1 %in% stop_words$word,
      !word2 %in% stop_words$word
    ) |>
    unite(word, word1, word2, sep = " ")
}
dogville_tokenize <- tokenize_text(clean_dogville) 
dogville_tokenize <- dogville_tokenize |>
  filter(
    !str_detect(
      word,
      "\\b(film|movie|dogville|trier|von|kidman|nicole|lars|paul|bettany|lauren|bacall|james|caan|john|hurt|patricia|clarkson|ben|gazzara)\\b"
    )
  )
```


# Term Frequencies

```{r}
term_frequencies <- function(data) {
  data |>
    count(word, sort = TRUE) |>
    slice_max(n, n = 8) |>
    mutate(word = reorder(word, n)) |>
    ggplot(aes(y = word, x = n)) +
    geom_col() +
    labs(
      title = "Top 8 Words",
      x = "Count"
    )
}
```

```{r}
dog_ville_frequencies <- term_frequencies(dogville_tokenize)
dog_ville_frequencies
```

# Sentiment Analysis

```{r}
sentiment_wordcloud <- function(data, lexicon = "bing") {
  sentiment_words <- get_sentiments(lexicon)

  data |>
    inner_join(sentiment_words, by = "word") |>
    count(word, sentiment, sort = TRUE) |>
    mutate(color = if_else(sentiment == "negative",
    "#C0392B",
    "#2E7D32")) |>
    with(
      wordcloud(
        words = word,
        freq = n,
        colors = color,
        ordered.colors = TRUE,
        max.words = 100
      )
    )
}
```

```{r}
# separating positive and negative
sentiment_wordcloud <- function(data, lexicon = "bing") {
  sentiment_counts <- data |>
    inner_join(get_sentiments(lexicon), by = "word") |>
    count(word, sentiment, sort = TRUE)

  par(mfrow = c(2, 1))

  sentiment_counts |>
    filter(sentiment == "positive") |>
    with(
      wordcloud(
        word,
        n,
        colors = "#2E7D32",
        max.words = 50
      )
    )

  sentiment_counts |>
    filter(sentiment == "negative") |>
    with(
      wordcloud(
        word,
        n,
        colors = "#C0392B",
        max.words = 50
      )
    )

  par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
}
```

```{r}
sentiment_wordcloud <- function(data, lexicon = "bing") {
  sentiment_words <- get_sentiments(lexicon)

  sentiment_matrix <- data |>
    separate(word, into = c("word1", "word2"), sep = " ", remove = FALSE) |>
    left_join(
      sentiment_words,
      by = c("word1" = "word")
    ) |>
    rename(sentiment1 = sentiment) |>
    left_join(
      sentiment_words,
      by = c("word2" = "word")
    ) |>
    rename(sentiment2 = sentiment) |>
    mutate(
      sentiment = case_when(
        !is.na(sentiment1) & is.na(sentiment2) ~ sentiment1,
        is.na(sentiment1) & !is.na(sentiment2) ~ sentiment2,
        sentiment1 == sentiment2 ~ sentiment1,
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(sentiment)) |>
    count(word, sentiment) |>
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = sentiment,
      values_from = n,
      values_fill = 0
    ) |>
    column_to_rownames("word") |>
    as.matrix()

  comparison.cloud(
    sentiment_matrix,
    colors = c("#C0392B", "#2E7D32"),
    max.words = 100
  )
}
```



```{r}
dogville_sentiment <- sentiment_wordcloud(dogville_tokenize)
```


# Choosing K

```{r}
plot_k_diagnostics <- function(search_k) {
  search_k$results |>
    select(K, exclus, semcoh, heldout, residual) |>
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -K,
      names_to = "metric",
      values_to = "value"
    ) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = K, y = value)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(
      title = "Model Diagnostics by Number of Topics",
      x = "Number of Topics"
    )
}
```


```{r}
library(stm)
dogville_dtm <- dogville_tokenize |>
  count(row_id, word) |>
  cast_sparse(row_id, word, n)

dogville_stm <- readCorpus(dogville_dtm, type = "Matrix")

dogville_search_k <- searchK(
  documents = dogville_stm$documents,
  vocab = dogville_stm$vocab,
  K = 2:10
)

dogville_search_k <- searchK(
  dogville_dtm,
  K = 2:5
)

dogville_k_diagnostics <- plot_k_diagnostics(dogville_search_k)
```

```{r}
dogville_stm <- readCorpus(
  as.matrix(dogville_dtm),
  type = "dtm"
)

dogville_search_k <- searchK(
  documents = dogville_stm$documents,
  vocab = dogville_stm$vocab,
  K = 2:10
)
```


```{r}
plot_k_diagnostics <- function(data, k = 2:10) {
  heldout <- make.heldout(data)

  tibble(K = k) |>
    mutate(
      topic_model = map(K, ~ stm(data, K = .x, verbose = FALSE)),
      semantic_coherence = map(topic_model, semanticCoherence, data),
      eval_heldout = map(topic_model, eval.heldout, heldout$missing),
      residual = map(topic_model, checkResiduals, data),
      bound = map_dbl(topic_model, ~ max(.x$convergence$bound)),
      lbound = bound + map_dbl(topic_model, ~ lfactorial(.x$settings$dim$K))
    ) |>
    transmute(
      K,
      `Lower bound` = lbound,
      Residuals = map_dbl(residual, "dispersion"),
      `Semantic coherence` = map_dbl(semantic_coherence, mean),
      `Held-out likelihood` = map_dbl(eval_heldout, "expected.heldout")
    ) |>
    pivot_longer(-K, names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") |>
    ggplot(aes(K, Value)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    facet_wrap(~ Metric, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(
      x = "K (number of topics)",
      y = NULL,
      title = "Model diagnostics by number of topics"
    )
}
```

```{r}
dogville_dtm <- dogville_tokenize |>
  count(row_id, word) |>
  cast_sparse(row_id, word, n)

dogville_stm <- readCorpus(
  as.matrix(dogville_dtm),
  type = "dtm"
)

plot_k_diagnostics(dogville_dtm)
```

# Topic Model

```{r}
model_data <- function(preped_data, k = 8) {
  fit <- stm(
    documents = preped_data$documents,
    vocab = preped_data$vocab,
    K = k,
    init.type = "Spectral"
  )

  top_terms <- tidy(fit, matrix = "beta") |>
    group_by(topic) |>
    slice_max(beta, n = 7, with_ties = FALSE) |>
    summarise(terms = paste(term, collapse = ", "))

  tidy(fit, matrix = "gamma") |>
    group_by(topic) |>
    summarise(gamma = mean(gamma)) |>
    left_join(top_terms, by = "topic") |>
    mutate(topic = reorder(paste("Topic", topic), gamma)) |>
    ggplot(aes(topic, gamma, label = terms)) +
    geom_col() +
    geom_text(hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.002, size = 3) +
    coord_flip(clip = "off") +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = scales::percent,
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.7))
    ) +
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = "Average topic prevalence",
      title = "Topic Prevalence and Top Terms"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
}
```

```{r}
model_data <- function(preped_data, k = 8) {
  fit <- stm(
    documents = preped_data$documents,
    vocab = preped_data$vocab,
    K = k,
    init.type = "Spectral"
  )

  top_terms <- tidy(fit, matrix = "beta") |>
    group_by(topic) |>
    slice_max(beta, n = 7, with_ties = FALSE) |>
    summarise(terms = paste(term, collapse = ", "))

  tidy(fit, matrix = "gamma") |>
    group_by(topic) |>
    summarise(gamma = mean(gamma)) |>
    left_join(top_terms, by = "topic") |>
    mutate(topic = reorder(paste("Topic", topic), gamma)) |>
    ggplot(aes(topic, gamma, label = terms)) +
    geom_col() +
    geom_text(hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.002, size = 3) +
    coord_flip(clip = "off") +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = scales::percent,
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.7))
    ) +
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = "Average topic prevalence",
      title = "Topic Prevalence and Top Terms"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
} 
```


```{r}
dogville_model <- model_data(dogville_stm, k = 7)

dogville_model
```
